audit.similarity_method: E5 semantic (passage:), threshold 0.93, MODERN re-voiced texts

lessons.text: Notice fear grip your body before a hard task.
lessons.rationale: Captures Arjuna's visceral panic—failing limbs, parched mouth, trembling, the bow slipping—as the felt onset of fear before an overwhelming task, not an abstract mood.
lessons.old_text: Notice when dread seizes your body before a hard duty.

lessons.text: Some wins bring only guilt.
lessons.rationale: Anchors to Arjuna's insight that slaying his kin yields no good and only sin—distinct from the fear lesson because it judges the outcome of the act, not the emotion before it.
lessons.old_text: Some victories bring only guilt, never happiness.

lessons.text: No prize is worth ruining people you love.
lessons.rationale: Specific to Arjuna's rejection of kingdom and pleasures once he sees that the very people he'd win them for stand before him as the cost—about the worthlessness of the reward itself.
lessons.old_text: Refuse a prize bought with the ruin of those you love.

lessons.text: Others' greed is no excuse for your own.
lessons.rationale: Draws on Arjuna contrasting the enemy's greed-blinded conscience with his own clear sight, and his admission of being swept toward sin by greed—about moral responsibility despite what others excuse.
lessons.old_text: When greed blinds others to wrong, still choose to turn away.

lessons.text: One reckless act can wound generations.
lessons.rationale: Captures Arjuna's argument that destroying a family collapses its traditions, order, and lineage far beyond the battlefield—distinct as consequence spreading outward through time and society.
lessons.old_text: Weigh how one destructive act can unravel generations.

lessons.text: Don't let despair talk you into giving up.
lessons.rationale: Isolates the moment Arjuna would rather be killed unresisting than fight—the paralysis where grief distorts judgment into passive collapse, distinct from his earlier reasoned objections.
lessons.old_text: Beware letting despair make surrender feel preferable to acting.

lessons.text: Drop the self-pity and stand up.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's opening rebuke to shake off paralyzing despondency and stand up.
lessons.old_text: Cast off weakness of heart and rise to act.

lessons.text: When you're lost, ask someone wiser.
lessons.rationale: Captures Arjuna surrendering as a disciple asking for instruction, distinct from the despair verses around it.
lessons.old_text: When your duty confuses you, seek a wise teacher.

lessons.text: Don't let grief run your life.
lessons.rationale: The opening thesis of the whole discourse: grief itself is the error.
lessons.old_text: The wise grieve for neither the living nor the dead.

lessons.text: You remain yourself through every stage of life.
lessons.rationale: Anchors to the specific image of passing through life's stages into another body.
lessons.old_text: The self endures unchanged through youth, age, and death.

lessons.text: Pleasure and pain both pass; ride them out.
lessons.rationale: Sense-contact brings impermanent heat and cold; the one who endures them is fit for immortality.
lessons.old_text: Bear pleasure and pain evenly; both come and go.

lessons.text: Grief can't undo what was always coming.
lessons.rationale: The alternative argument: even granting mortality, death is certain, so grief is pointless.
lessons.old_text: Even were death final, grief cannot undo the inevitable.

lessons.text: Don't shrink from what's yours to do.
lessons.rationale: Duty framed as obligation and opportunity, with abandonment counted as sin.
lessons.old_text: Do your rightful duty rather than shrink from it.

lessons.text: Running from your work costs your good name.
lessons.rationale: The worldly reputation argument, distinct from the duty-as-sin appeal before it.
lessons.old_text: Fleeing your duty brings dishonor worse than death.

lessons.text: Treat win and loss the same, then act.
lessons.rationale: The pivot verse equalizing all outcomes before acting.
lessons.old_text: Meet victory and defeat, gain and loss, alike.

lessons.text: No honest effort is ever wasted.
lessons.rationale: Introduces Yoga of action: no loss, and a little of it guards from great fear.
lessons.old_text: On this path no effort is ever wasted.

lessons.text: One clear aim beats a dozen scattered ones.
lessons.rationale: Single-pointed determination against the many-branched thoughts of the irresolute.
lessons.old_text: Hold one clear resolve; scattered aims lead nowhere.

lessons.text: Chasing every reward keeps your mind restless.
lessons.rationale: Those enchanted by flowery promises of enjoyment never reach steady concentration.
lessons.old_text: Chasing pleasure and reward leaves the mind unsettled.

lessons.text: Don't be ruled by highs, lows, or wanting more.
lessons.rationale: Transcend the three gunas, opposites, and the urge to acquire and hoard.
lessons.old_text: Rise above the pairs of opposites and possessiveness.

lessons.text: Direct understanding outgrows secondhand answers.
lessons.rationale: The reservoir-in-a-flood image: direct knowing makes borrowed texts superfluous.
lessons.old_text: To the awakened, scripture is a well amid a flood.

lessons.text: Your right is to your work, not its rewards.
lessons.rationale: The keystone teaching on action without claim to the fruit.
lessons.old_text: You have a right to your work, never its results.

lessons.text: Stay steady through both success and failure.
lessons.rationale: Defines yoga specifically as evenness of mind while acting.
lessons.old_text: Even-mindedness in success and failure is true yoga.

lessons.text: Skill is working wisely, not just working hard.
lessons.rationale: Wise action transcends good and evil, far above work driven by reward.
lessons.old_text: Ground action in wisdom; that is skill in action.

lessons.text: Let go of the payoff and you're free.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing fruits specifically frees one from the bonds of rebirth.
lessons.old_text: Release the fruits of action and be freed from bondage.

lessons.text: See clearly and empty talk loses its pull.
lessons.rationale: Passing the mire of delusion brings indifference to what is heard and to be heard.
lessons.old_text: Cross beyond delusion and lose your taste for empty words.

lessons.text: Clarity comes when your mind stops wavering.
lessons.rationale: Steadiness of intellect in the self, not scripture, yields realization.
lessons.old_text: When your mind rests unmoved in the self, you awaken.

lessons.text: Real contentment comes from within, not from getting.
lessons.rationale: First mark of steady wisdom: casting off desires and being satisfied in the self.
lessons.old_text: True contentment is found within, not in fulfilled desires.

lessons.text: Stay steady through sorrow, craving, and fear.
lessons.rationale: The sage undisturbed by adversity and released from attachment, fear, and anger.
lessons.old_text: Stay unshaken by sorrow and free of craving and fear.

lessons.text: Don't cling to good news or flinch at bad.
lessons.rationale: Firm wisdom neither rejoices at fortune nor hurries from misfortune.
lessons.old_text: Neither celebrate the good nor recoil from the bad.

lessons.text: Pull your attention back when the world overwhelms.
lessons.rationale: The signature tortoise image for sense-withdrawal.
lessons.old_text: Withdraw your senses as a tortoise draws in its limbs.

lessons.text: Willpower stops the act; insight ends the craving.
lessons.rationale: Objects leave the abstinent but the underlying longing fades only on seeing the Supreme.
lessons.old_text: Abstaining curbs the act; only insight ends the craving.

lessons.text: Even the disciplined get pulled off by impulse.
lessons.rationale: A warning that turbulent senses overpower even one striving to control them.
lessons.old_text: Even the wise can be swept away by restless senses.

lessons.text: Master your impulses and keep your aim high.
lessons.rationale: Restraint paired with devotion to the divine steadies wisdom.
lessons.old_text: Master your senses and fix your mind on the highest.

lessons.text: Dwelling on wanting breeds desire, then anger, then ruin.
lessons.rationale: The famous descent chain from brooding to attachment, anger, delusion, and destruction.
lessons.old_text: Dwelling on objects breeds desire, then anger, then ruin.

lessons.text: Meet temptation calmly, neither grabbing nor fleeing.
lessons.rationale: The self-controlled act in the world free of attraction and repulsion, ending all pain.
lessons.old_text: Move among temptations without craving or aversion to find peace.

lessons.text: No peace or joy without a steady mind.
lessons.rationale: The negative chain: no wisdom, meditation, peace, or happiness for the unsteady.
lessons.old_text: Without a steady mind there is no peace or joy.

lessons.text: One stray impulse drags your focus off course.
lessons.rationale: The wind-and-boat image: one loose sense drags away discrimination, so restrain them all.
lessons.old_text: Rein in the wandering senses that pull the mind off course.

lessons.text: What everyone chases, the wise often question.
lessons.rationale: The night-and-day inversion of the sage's values against the world's.
lessons.old_text: What the world chases, the wise quietly turn from.

lessons.text: Let desires come and go like rivers to the sea.
lessons.rationale: The ocean image: peace comes to one whom desires enter without disturbing.
lessons.old_text: Let desires enter and pass like rivers into the sea.

lessons.text: Let go of craving, ownership, and ego.
lessons.rationale: Peace through abandoning longing, the sense of 'mine,' and egoism.
lessons.old_text: Drop craving, ownership, and ego to arrive at peace.

lessons.text: True peace holds steady even at the end.
lessons.rationale: The concluding Brahmic state, undeluded and attained even at life's end.
lessons.old_text: Reach this state and even death cannot delude you.

lessons.text: You can't not act, so act well.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing deeds brings neither actionlessness nor perfection; Nature compels everyone to act ceaselessly.
lessons.old_text: You cannot escape action, even for a moment.

lessons.text: Don't fake discipline while your mind wanders.
lessons.rationale: Stilling the limbs while brooding on sense-objects is hypocrisy; true yoga controls the senses and still acts.
lessons.old_text: Don't fake restraint while your mind chases pleasures.

lessons.text: Doing something beats doing nothing.
lessons.rationale: Perform your bounden duty, for even sustaining the body is impossible through inaction.
lessons.old_text: Do your appointed work; action beats inaction.

lessons.text: Work as a contribution, not just for gain.
lessons.rationale: Work done for sacrifice frees you; all other action binds. Act for that sake alone, free of attachment.
lessons.old_text: Perform every action as an offering, not for gain.

lessons.text: Give back to whatever sustains you.
lessons.rationale: Mutual nourishment sustains creation; one who enjoys the gods' gifts without offering back is a thief, and who cooks for himself alone consumes sin.
lessons.old_text: Repay what sustains you; take nothing for free.

lessons.text: Keep giving back, or your life runs empty.
lessons.rationale: Food, rain, sacrifice, and action form one turning wheel rooted in Brahma; whoever refuses to keep it moving lives pointlessly.
lessons.old_text: Keep the wheel of giving turning, or live in vain.

lessons.text: When you're whole within, you need nothing to prove.
lessons.rationale: One who rejoices and rests wholly in the Self has nothing to gain from deeds and depends on no being.
lessons.old_text: The self-fulfilled need nothing from action or others.

lessons.text: Do what must be done without clinging.
lessons.rationale: Always do what must be done detached; by unattached action one attains the Supreme.
lessons.old_text: Work without attachment, and you reach the Supreme.

lessons.text: People copy what their leaders actually do.
lessons.rationale: Janaka reached perfection through action; whatever a great person does and establishes, the world imitates.
lessons.old_text: Set the standard; the world follows its leaders.

lessons.text: Don't quit just because you've nothing left to gain.
lessons.rationale: Krishna has nothing left to attain yet acts, because if he stopped, people would falter and the worlds would fall into ruin.
lessons.old_text: Keep working though you need nothing, for the world's sake.

lessons.text: Do good calmly, without unsettling others.
lessons.rationale: The wise act unattached wishing the world's welfare, and must not disturb the minds of those still attached to action.
lessons.old_text: Work detached for others' good, without unsettling them.

lessons.text: Ease up — you're not the only force at work.
lessons.rationale: Egoism alone claims doership; one who knows the gunas merely move among gunas stays unattached.
lessons.old_text: Nature does the work; drop 'I am the doer.'

lessons.text: Act freely, without ego or feverish worry.
lessons.rationale: Surrender every act to the Divine, mind fixed on the Self, free of hope, ego, and mental fever.
lessons.old_text: Offer all your actions to the Divine and fight.

lessons.text: Follow good guidance with trust, not cynicism.
lessons.rationale: Those who follow it with faith are freed from bondage; those who criticize and ignore it are lost.
lessons.old_text: Practice this teaching with faith, never scorn.

lessons.text: Don't let likes and dislikes run your choices.
lessons.rationale: Even the wise follow their nature; attachment and aversion seated in the senses are enemies whose sway you must refuse.
lessons.old_text: Never let attraction and aversion rule you.

lessons.text: Better your own path flawed than another's perfect.
lessons.rationale: One's own duty, even flawed, surpasses another's done perfectly; another's path is fraught with fear.
lessons.old_text: Do your own duty poorly rather than another's well.

lessons.text: Desire and anger are your real enemies.
lessons.rationale: What drives a person to sin against his will is desire and anger, born of rajas, all-devouring foes.
lessons.old_text: Know desire and anger as your real enemy.

lessons.text: Craving clouds judgment like smoke hides fire.
lessons.rationale: Insatiable desire, seated in senses, mind, and intellect, envelops wisdom as smoke covers fire and dust a mirror.
lessons.old_text: Desire veils your wisdom like smoke hides fire.

lessons.text: First control the impulse, then uproot the craving.
lessons.rationale: Control the senses first, then destroy desire; knowing the Self is highest, restrain the self by the Self and conquer this foe.
lessons.old_text: Master your senses, then slay desire by the Self.

lessons.text: Stand on the wisdom of those before you.
lessons.rationale: The yoga passed from teacher to student in unbroken succession, revived when lost.
lessons.old_text: Receive sacred wisdom handed down through teachers.

lessons.text: Step up to set things right when they slip.
lessons.rationale: The divine descends age after age to protect the good and renew righteousness.
lessons.old_text: Restore what is right whenever it declines.

lessons.text: See clearly, and you stop repeating the same mistakes.
lessons.rationale: Seeing the divine birth and actions in their true light frees one from returning.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine truly and end rebirth.

lessons.text: Let understanding dissolve your fear and anger.
lessons.rationale: Those purified by the fire of knowledge and freed of these three attain the divine.
lessons.old_text: Burn attachment, fear, and anger in knowledge.

lessons.text: People meet you the way you approach them.
lessons.rationale: In whatever way people approach the divine, so are they answered.
lessons.old_text: Others meet you the way you approach them.

lessons.text: If you want results, act—don't just wish.
lessons.rationale: Those who long for results act and sacrifice, for success comes quickly through action.
lessons.old_text: Take decisive action when you seek worldly success.

lessons.text: Do the work without letting it define you.
lessons.rationale: The Self is the non-doer, untainted and free of desire for the fruits of action.
lessons.old_text: Work knowing action never taints the Self.

lessons.text: Know when to act and when to hold back.
lessons.rationale: Even the wise are confused about action and inaction; understanding them liberates.
lessons.old_text: Study action and inaction to win freedom.

lessons.text: Even doing nothing is a choice you're making.
lessons.rationale: The one who perceives this is wise, a yogi amid all his doing.
lessons.old_text: See inaction in action and action in inaction.

lessons.text: Act from clarity, not from craving.
lessons.rationale: The sage's undertakings are free of selfish desire, consumed by the fire of knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Let knowledge burn desire from your work.

lessons.text: Act without clinging to how it turns out.
lessons.rationale: Abandoning attachment to fruits and controlling the mind, one acts yet incurs no sin.
lessons.old_text: Drop hope and craving while you act.

lessons.text: Be content with what comes; chase nothing.
lessons.rationale: Content with what comes unsought and free of opposites, one acts unbound.
lessons.old_text: Stay even-minded in success and failure.

lessons.text: Selfless work leaves no weight behind.
lessons.rationale: For the detached mind fixed in knowledge, action done as sacrifice melts away entirely.
lessons.old_text: Work for sacrifice and your karma dissolves.

lessons.text: Bring your full presence to whatever you do.
lessons.rationale: Brahman is the offering, the fire, and the doer for one who sees it in all action.
lessons.old_text: See the divine present in every act.

lessons.text: Master your impulses instead of obeying them.
lessons.rationale: Diverse yogis sacrifice senses, objects, breath, and study in the fire of restraint.
lessons.old_text: Offer your senses and breath as sacrifice.

lessons.text: Give something of yourself, or gain nothing.
lessons.rationale: This world is not for the non-sacrificer; those who share the remnants reach the eternal.
lessons.old_text: Give of yourself, for without sacrifice nothing is gained.

lessons.text: Nothing worthwhile comes without effort.
lessons.rationale: Recognizing all sacrifices as born of action, one is liberated.
lessons.old_text: Know every sacrifice is born of action.

lessons.text: Understanding matters more than going through the motions.
lessons.rationale: Knowledge-sacrifice is superior to material sacrifice; every action culminates in knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Prize wisdom above ritual; all action ends in knowledge.

lessons.text: Ask, listen, and stay humble enough to learn.
lessons.rationale: The realized teach knowledge to those who prostrate, question, and serve.
lessons.old_text: Approach the wise with humility and service.

lessons.text: See yourself in others, and the walls come down.
lessons.rationale: With this knowledge you perceive all beings in yourself and in the divine.
lessons.old_text: See every being within your own Self.

lessons.text: Understanding can carry you past any mistake.
lessons.rationale: However sinful, one crosses over all sin by knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Cross even the worst sins by the raft of knowledge.

lessons.text: Insight loosens the grip of the past.
lessons.rationale: Like fire turning fuel to ash, the fire of knowledge consumes all actions.
lessons.old_text: Let knowledge reduce your karma to ash.

lessons.text: Nothing clears you like real understanding.
lessons.rationale: There is no purifier in this world equal to knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Nothing purifies you like true knowledge.

lessons.text: Commit fully, stay disciplined, and peace follows.
lessons.rationale: The faithful, devoted, and sense-controlled attain knowledge and then supreme peace.
lessons.old_text: Pair faith with restraint to gain lasting peace.

lessons.text: Endless doubt drains every joy.
lessons.rationale: The ignorant and doubting perish, finding no happiness in this world or the next.
lessons.old_text: Release doubt, which destroys your happiness.

lessons.text: Cut through your doubts and get moving.
lessons.rationale: Slay the doubt born of ignorance with self-knowledge, take refuge in yoga, and act.
lessons.old_text: Cut doubt with the sword of knowledge and rise.

lessons.text: Better to engage than to withdraw.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's verdict: renunciation and karma yoga both lead to bliss, but active yoga is superior.
lessons.old_text: Choose engaged action over withdrawal; both reach the goal.

lessons.text: Free yourself from both craving and resentment.
lessons.rationale: The true renunciate is free from the pairs of opposites, easily liberated.
lessons.old_text: Neither crave nor hate, and bondage falls away.

lessons.text: Thinking and doing are one path, not two.
lessons.rationale: Only the childish separate Sankhya and Karma Yoga; whoever masters one gains both.
lessons.old_text: See knowledge and action as one path, not two.

lessons.text: Start by doing; wisdom grows from practice.
lessons.rationale: Renunciation is hard without yoga; the yoga-harmonized sage quickly reaches Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Ground yourself in action first; renunciation ripens from it.

lessons.text: Master yourself first, then act freely.
lessons.rationale: The self-conquered doer who sees the Self in all is not stained by action.
lessons.old_text: Purify your mind and subdue the senses; then act untainted.

lessons.text: Notice your reactions instead of becoming them.
lessons.rationale: The knower sees seeing, eating, breathing as sense activity, not the Self acting.
lessons.old_text: Know the senses move among objects; you do nothing.

lessons.text: Do your part, then let results roll off you.
lessons.rationale: Actions offered to Brahman without attachment leave no taint, like water on a lotus leaf.
lessons.old_text: Offer your work to the divine and stay unstained like a lotus leaf.

lessons.text: Work to grow, not just to gain.
lessons.rationale: Yogis act without attachment through body, mind, and senses for self-purification.
lessons.old_text: Work with body and mind to purify yourself, not to gain.

lessons.text: Let go of the payoff and find peace.
lessons.rationale: Abandoning the fruit of action brings peace; desire for it brings bondage.
lessons.old_text: Release the fruits and rest in lasting peace.

lessons.text: Stay inwardly still even while you act.
lessons.rationale: Mentally renouncing all action, the self-controlled one rests happily in the body, neither acting nor causing action.
lessons.old_text: Renounce action in the mind and dwell content within.

lessons.text: Don't take all the credit or all the blame.
lessons.rationale: The Lord creates no agency or action; nature acts, and ignorance alone deludes beings into taking blame or credit.
lessons.old_text: Attribute doing to nature; the Self is no agent.

lessons.text: Understanding clears confusion like sunrise.
lessons.rationale: Knowledge destroys ignorance and reveals the Supreme, as the sun reveals what darkness hid.
lessons.old_text: Let self-knowledge burn away ignorance like the sun.

lessons.text: Give yourself fully to what matters most.
lessons.rationale: Intellect, self, and goal fixed on Brahman lead to the state of no return, sins dispelled by knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Absorb your whole being in the Supreme and never return.

lessons.text: Treat everyone as equally worthy.
lessons.rationale: Sages see the same Self in scholar, cow, elephant, dog, and outcaste alike.
lessons.old_text: Look with an equal eye on every being.

lessons.text: Don't swing with every high and low.
lessons.rationale: The knower of Brahman neither rejoices at the pleasant nor grieves at the unpleasant.
lessons.old_text: Stay steady, unmoved by the pleasant or the unpleasant.

lessons.text: Find joy within, not in things outside you.
lessons.rationale: Unattached to external contact, one finds endless happiness in the Self.
lessons.old_text: Find your joy in the Self, not in outer contact.

lessons.text: Chasing quick pleasures ends in pain.
lessons.rationale: Enjoyments born of contact have a beginning and end and are wombs of pain; the wise avoid them.
lessons.old_text: Shun fleeting pleasures; they begin and end in pain.

lessons.text: Ride out desire and anger before they rule you.
lessons.rationale: Withstanding the impulse of desire and anger makes a happy yogi; happy and illumined within, he attains Moksha.
lessons.old_text: Conquer the surge of desire and anger to find joy within.

lessons.text: Work for the good of others.
lessons.rationale: Sages gain absolute freedom being self-controlled and intent on the good of all beings.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself to the welfare of all beings.

lessons.text: A quiet mind sets you free.
lessons.rationale: Freedom surrounds the self-controlled who have mastered their thoughts and realized the Self.
lessons.old_text: Still your thoughts, and freedom opens on every side.

lessons.text: Steady your breath to steady your focus.
lessons.rationale: The meditation technique: shut out contacts, fix the gaze between the brows, and balance the breath.
lessons.old_text: Steady your gaze and breath to turn attention inward.

lessons.text: Govern your mind, and fear loses its grip.
lessons.rationale: With senses and mind ever controlled and free from desire, fear, and anger, the sage is liberated.
lessons.old_text: Control senses, mind, and intellect, and be forever free.

lessons.text: See the world as friendly, and rest easier.
lessons.rationale: Knowing the Lord as enjoyer of offerings and friend of all beings brings peace.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine as friend of all, and find peace.

lessons.text: Do what's yours to do, results aside.
lessons.rationale: Defines the true renouncer as one who acts without depending on fruits, not one who merely abstains.
lessons.old_text: Do your duty without clinging to results.

lessons.text: Drop the selfish motive, not the work.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is renunciation of self-centered thoughts and attachment, reached through action then settling into inaction.
lessons.old_text: Renounce selfish intentions, not action itself.

lessons.text: Lift yourself up; don't let yourself sink.
lessons.rationale: Raise yourself by your own self; do not let yourself sink.
lessons.old_text: Lift yourself by your own effort.

lessons.text: Be your own ally, not your own worst critic.
lessons.rationale: The conquered self befriends you; the unconquered self acts as your foe.
lessons.old_text: Be your own friend, not your own enemy.

lessons.text: Stay level through praise and blame alike.
lessons.rationale: The self-controlled remain even in pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.
lessons.old_text: Stay balanced through cold and heat, praise and blame.

lessons.text: Gold or dirt—don't let either move you.
lessons.rationale: The content yogi treats a clod, a stone, and gold as the same.
lessons.old_text: Value wisdom over gold and stone alike.

lessons.text: Meet friend and rival with the same calm.
lessons.rationale: Excellence is being even-minded toward the loving, the hostile, and the indifferent.
lessons.old_text: Look on friend and foe with the same eye.

lessons.text: Spend quiet time alone, free of wanting.
lessons.rationale: Strive constantly to steady the mind, alone, with body and mind controlled.
lessons.old_text: Practice in solitude, free from hope and greed.

lessons.text: Set up a simple, steady space to focus.
lessons.rationale: Practical instruction on preparing a stable place to sit.
lessons.old_text: Set a firm, clean seat, neither high nor low.

lessons.text: Focus on one thing to clear your mind.
lessons.rationale: Seated, gather the mind and senses to a single point for inner purification.
lessons.old_text: Make the mind one-pointed to purify yourself.

lessons.text: Sit tall and still to settle the mind.
lessons.rationale: Concrete posture instruction: erect, steady, gaze unwandering.
lessons.old_text: Hold your body, head, and neck straight and still.

lessons.text: Sit calm and fearless, focused on your aim.
lessons.rationale: Calm, fearless, disciplined, with the highest as the supreme aim.
lessons.old_text: Sit serene and fearless, mind fixed on the goal.

lessons.text: A steady mind brings lasting calm.
lessons.rationale: Keeping the mind balanced culminates in the peace of liberation.
lessons.old_text: A steadied mind leads you to lasting peace.

lessons.text: Eat, sleep, and work in moderation.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is impossible with too much or too little food and sleep; moderation destroys pain.
lessons.old_text: Eat and sleep in moderation, never in extremes.

lessons.text: Let the mind rest, free of wanting.
lessons.rationale: Union is the controlled mind resting in the Self, free from all longing.
lessons.old_text: Rest the mind in the Self, free of craving.

lessons.text: Keep your focus steady as a sheltered flame.
lessons.rationale: The steady yogi's mind is like a flame where no wind blows.
lessons.old_text: Keep your mind unflickering like a sheltered lamp.

lessons.text: Contentment comes from knowing yourself.
lessons.rationale: When the mind quiets, one sees the Self by the Self and is satisfied.
lessons.old_text: Grow content by seeing the Self within yourself.

lessons.text: Find a joy the senses can't give or take.
lessons.rationale: Infinite bliss grasped by the intellect, transcending the senses, holds one steady.
lessons.old_text: Anchor yourself in bliss beyond the senses.

lessons.text: Find inner steadiness, and grief can't topple you.
lessons.rationale: Established in this gain, one is unmoved even by heavy grief.
lessons.old_text: Once you find it, no sorrow can shake you.

lessons.text: Practice with resolve and without despair.
lessons.rationale: Yoga, the severance from pain, is to be practiced with determination and without despair.
lessons.old_text: Practice with resolve and an undespairing mind.

lessons.text: Drop the wants your imagination invents.
lessons.rationale: Abandon all desires from mental fancy and restrain the senses from every side.
lessons.old_text: Let go of every desire born of imagination.

lessons.text: Quiet the mind gradually, one step at a time.
lessons.rationale: Little by little, steady the intellect and fix the mind on the Self alone.
lessons.old_text: Quiet the mind gradually, one step at a time.

lessons.text: When your mind wanders, gently bring it back.
lessons.rationale: From wherever the restless mind strays, draw it back under the Self's control.
lessons.old_text: Whenever the mind wanders, gently bring it back.

lessons.text: A calm mind opens the way to deep joy.
lessons.rationale: The yogi of peaceful mind, free from passion, enjoys the infinite bliss of Brahman.
lessons.old_text: A calmed mind opens the door to supreme bliss.

lessons.text: See yourself in everyone, and never feel alone.
lessons.rationale: The harmonized mind sees the Self everywhere and never feels separate.
lessons.old_text: See yourself in all beings and all beings in you.

lessons.text: Feel others' joy and pain as your own.
lessons.rationale: The highest yogi sees all through the likeness of the Self, in pleasure and pain alike.
lessons.old_text: Feel others' joy and pain as your own.

lessons.text: Admit it: the mind is restless and hard to hold.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna names the mind restless, turbulent, and as hard to control as the wind.
lessons.old_text: Admit the mind is restless and hard to hold.

lessons.text: Tame a restless mind with practice and letting go.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's answer: the difficult mind is restrained by practice and dispassion.
lessons.old_text: Tame the restless mind through practice and detachment.

lessons.text: Self-control makes the impossible reachable.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is hard for the uncontrolled but reachable by the striving, self-controlled one.
lessons.old_text: Self-mastery makes the seemingly impossible attainable.

lessons.text: No honest effort is ever wasted.
lessons.rationale: The one who falls from yoga does not perish; none who does good comes to grief.
lessons.old_text: No sincere effort toward good is ever wasted.

lessons.text: What you build now carries into what's next.
lessons.rationale: The fallen yogi is reborn favorably and reconnects with former practice, striving further.
lessons.old_text: Every good beginning carries forward into your future.

lessons.text: Keep going; progress compounds over time.
lessons.rationale: The yogi who strives on, perfected gradually, reaches the highest goal.
lessons.old_text: Persist across setbacks; progress compounds over time.

lessons.text: Steady practice beats just knowing about it.
lessons.rationale: The yogi surpasses ascetics, scholars, and ritualists; devoted practice with faith is highest.
lessons.old_text: Choose disciplined practice over mere knowledge or ritual.

lessons.text: Don't just learn it. Live it.
lessons.rationale: Verses 1-2 promise knowledge combined with realization, after which nothing remains to be known.
lessons.old_text: Pair knowledge with lived realization, not theory.

lessons.text: Real understanding is rare. Earn it patiently.
lessons.rationale: Among thousands who strive, only one truly knows the essence.
lessons.old_text: Accept that true realization is rare and hard-won.

lessons.text: You are more than your body and thoughts.
lessons.rationale: The eightfold material nature is inferior; the life-element is the higher nature that upholds the world.
lessons.old_text: Distinguish your body and mind from your living essence.

lessons.text: Everything is connected. Nothing stands alone.
lessons.rationale: All beings arise from the two natures; all things are strung on the divine like gems on a thread.
lessons.old_text: Trace every beginning and ending to one source.

lessons.text: Notice the wonder in ordinary things.
lessons.rationale: The divine is the flavor in water, light in sun and moon, fragrance in earth, life in all beings.
lessons.old_text: Notice the divine in taste, light, and sound.

lessons.text: Behind every talent is the same quiet spark.
lessons.rationale: The divine is the seed of all beings, intelligence of the intelligent, strength free of craving, desire aligned with dharma.
lessons.old_text: See the same essence behind every excellence.

lessons.text: Don't let a passing mood distort what's real.
lessons.rationale: The world is deluded by the three gunas; this divine illusion is crossed only by taking refuge.
lessons.old_text: See past the three moods that veil reality.

lessons.text: When judgment clouds, good people lose their way.
lessons.rationale: Those whose knowledge is destroyed by illusion follow lower ways and do not seek the divine.
lessons.old_text: Watch how delusion turns people from the good.

lessons.text: Seek meaning, not just relief or reward.
lessons.rationale: Four kinds seek the divine: the distressed, the curious, the wealth-seeker, and the wise.
lessons.old_text: Let your worship rise above distress and gain.

lessons.text: Stay steady on what matters most.
lessons.rationale: The wise, ever steadfast and devoted to the One, are dearest and held as the divine's own Self.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself with steady, single-minded love.

lessons.text: Look for something worthwhile in everyone you meet.
lessons.rationale: After many births the wise one realizes all this is the innermost Self; such a soul is rare.
lessons.old_text: Realize the divine dwells within everything you see.

lessons.text: Craving quietly steers you toward lesser things.
lessons.rationale: Those whose wisdom is stolen by desire turn to lesser gods, led by their own nature.
lessons.old_text: Watch how desire steers you toward lesser aims.

lessons.text: What you give attention to grows.
lessons.rationale: The divine makes firm whatever faith a devotee holds, and grants the outcome they seek.
lessons.old_text: Whatever you worship with faith is strengthened.

lessons.text: Chase what lasts, not what's quick.
lessons.rationale: The reward of small minds is finite; devotees of the highest reach the highest.
lessons.old_text: Choose lasting aims over quick, finite rewards.

lessons.text: Don't mistake the surface for the whole.
lessons.rationale: The foolish think the unmanifest is limited; the divine stays veiled by Yoga-Maya.
lessons.old_text: Do not mistake the formless for mere form.

lessons.text: Stay humble. No one is ever fully known.
lessons.rationale: The divine knows all beings past, present, and future, yet no one fully knows it.
lessons.old_text: Remember the knower is never fully known.

lessons.text: Don't be ruled by every like and dislike.
lessons.rationale: Beings fall into delusion through the pairs of opposites; the pure-hearted, freed from them, worship steadfastly.
lessons.old_text: Free yourself from the pull of like and dislike.

lessons.text: Something in you stays constant through every change.
lessons.rationale: Verses 1-4 answer Arjuna's questions by defining Brahman as the imperishable Supreme; the applicable teaching is to know the eternal Self behind perishable nature.
lessons.old_text: Know the imperishable Self behind all changing forms.

lessons.text: You become whatever you dwell on.
lessons.rationale: Both verses state that the being one dwells on at death is the state one attains.
lessons.old_text: Whatever you remember at the end, you become.

lessons.text: Keep what matters in mind while you work.
lessons.rationale: Krishna tells Arjuna to remember Him at all times and still fight — devotion woven through daily action.
lessons.old_text: Remember what matters most while you still act.

lessons.text: Steady the mind with daily practice.
lessons.rationale: The Supreme is reached through habitual meditation and a mind that does not wander.
lessons.old_text: Steady the mind through daily, unwavering practice.

lessons.text: Lift your mind toward something larger than yourself.
lessons.rationale: These verses describe meditating on the luminous, all-supporting Supreme beyond ignorance, with an unwavering devoted mind.
lessons.old_text: Contemplate what is vast, ancient, and beyond darkness.

lessons.text: Quiet the noise and turn inward.
lessons.rationale: Withdraw from external gates and confine the mind within to practice concentration.
lessons.old_text: Close the senses and gather your mind inward.

lessons.text: Anchor a restless mind on one word.
lessons.rationale: Uttering the single syllable Om steadies the departing mind toward the Supreme goal.
lessons.old_text: Anchor your restless mind to a single word.

lessons.text: Practiced daily, the hard thing grows easy.
lessons.rationale: The one who remembers single-mindedly and daily finds the Supreme easily attainable.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself constantly and the way grows easy.

lessons.text: Chase the lasting and step off the treadmill.
lessons.rationale: Those who attain the Supreme escape rebirth into this impermanent place of pain; even Brahma's world returns.
lessons.old_text: Reach the lasting, and leave suffering's cycle behind.

lessons.text: Everything built in time eventually dissolves.
lessons.rationale: The cosmic day-and-night cycle shows all manifest worlds and beings arise and dissolve repeatedly — the applicable lesson is the impermanence of all time-bound things.
lessons.old_text: Everything built in time will dissolve in time.

lessons.text: Invest in what outlasts you.
lessons.rationale: Beyond the dissolving unmanifest is an eternal one that is never destroyed — the highest, non-returning goal.
lessons.old_text: Seek the eternal that survives when all else ends.

lessons.text: Give your whole heart to one aim.
lessons.rationale: The highest is reached by unswerving, single-pointed devotion to the one who pervades all.
lessons.old_text: Give your whole heart to one undivided aim.

lessons.text: Move toward the light, not the smoke.
lessons.rationale: The two paths (bright leading to no return, dark leading to return) are largely cosmological; the extractable teaching is to choose the path of light. Verses 24-25's solstice/fortnight enumeration is technical.
lessons.old_text: Move toward the light, not toward the smoke.

lessons.text: Stay steady in practice through every season.
lessons.rationale: Knowing the paths, the yogi is never deluded and remains steadfast in yoga at all times.
lessons.old_text: Stay steadfast in practice through every season.

lessons.text: Inner work outlasts every outward show.
lessons.rationale: The yogi surpasses the merits of study, sacrifice, and austerity, reaching the Supreme Abode.
lessons.old_text: Inner practice outlasts every ritual and reward.

lessons.text: Meet new ideas without cynicism.
lessons.rationale: The secret is declared to one who does not cavil; open, uncynical receptivity is the prerequisite.
lessons.old_text: Approach sacred knowledge without cynicism or cavil.

lessons.text: The best path is often the simplest.
lessons.rationale: The royal science is called supremely pure yet very easy to perform and imperishable.
lessons.old_text: The highest path is the simplest to walk.

lessons.text: Without conviction, you drift back to the start.
lessons.rationale: Those lacking faith return to the worldly path without attaining the divine.
lessons.old_text: Without faith, you circle back where you started.

lessons.text: Be part of everything, attached to nothing.
lessons.rationale: All beings rest in the divine as wind rests in ether, yet it does not dwell in them; pervasion without attachment.
lessons.old_text: The divine pervades everything yet is bound by nothing.

lessons.text: What ends makes room for a new beginning.
lessons.rationale: Beings return to nature at the end of a cycle and are sent forth again; the rhythm of dissolution and renewal.
lessons.old_text: All that arises will dissolve and rise again.

lessons.text: Do the work without getting tangled in it.
lessons.rationale: The divine sits indifferent and unattached, so its acts do not bind; a model for acting without entanglement.
lessons.old_text: Act while resting untouched, like a witness.

lessons.text: Don't overlook greatness in ordinary packaging.
lessons.rationale: Fools disregard the divine clad in human form, and their hopes and deeds turn vain; a warning against shallow judgment.
lessons.old_text: Do not dismiss the sacred in ordinary form.

lessons.text: Do one thing at a time, completely.
lessons.rationale: Great souls worship with single-minded devotion, firm in their vows and constant in striving.
lessons.old_text: Worship with a single, undivided mind.

lessons.text: The same thread runs through everyone.
lessons.rationale: The divine is named as goal, shelter, mother, father, and seed of all; worship it as one yet manifold everywhere.
lessons.old_text: See the one divine behind every form.

lessons.text: Quick rewards run out. Deeper ones don't.
lessons.rationale: Vedic ritualists win heaven but return when their merit is exhausted, caught in coming and going.
lessons.old_text: Ritual rewards run out; the divine does not.

lessons.text: Commit fully, and you'll find what you need.
lessons.rationale: For those who worship with undivided minds, the divine secures what they lack and preserves what they have.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself fully, and your needs are carried.

lessons.text: Every sincere effort counts, whatever its form.
lessons.rationale: Even worship offered to other gods reaches the one divine; worshippers go to whatever they truly seek.
lessons.old_text: Every sincere prayer reaches the same source.

lessons.text: Given sincerely, even a small gift means everything.
lessons.rationale: A leaf, flower, fruit, or water offered with devotion by a pure heart is accepted; the sincerity matters, not the size.
lessons.old_text: Even a leaf, given with love, is enough.

lessons.text: Pour yourself fully into whatever you do.
lessons.rationale: Whatever you do, eat, give, or practice, do it as an offering to the divine.
lessons.old_text: Offer whatever you do as devotion.

lessons.text: Do the work, release the outcome.
lessons.rationale: Offering all deeds frees you from the bonds of good and evil fruits through the yoga of renunciation.
lessons.old_text: Offer your actions and their fruits lose their grip.

lessons.text: You get back the closeness you give.
lessons.rationale: None is hateful or dear, yet those who worship with devotion dwell in the divine and it in them.
lessons.old_text: The divine is equal to all, yet near to the devoted.

lessons.text: It's never too late to change direction.
lessons.rationale: Even the most sinful, once rightly resolved in devotion, soon becomes righteous; the devotee never perishes.
lessons.old_text: No one who truly turns back is beyond redemption.

lessons.text: The best in life is open to everyone.
lessons.rationale: All who take refuge, regardless of birth or station, attain the supreme goal.
lessons.old_text: The highest goal is open to everyone.

lessons.text: Show up fully: mind, heart, and effort.
lessons.rationale: The chapter closes with total self-offering: mind, devotion, sacrifice, and reverence united toward the supreme.
lessons.old_text: Fix your mind, bow, and give yourself wholly.

lessons.text: See clearly, and illusion loses its grip.
lessons.rationale: Verses 1-3 frame the chapter: knowing the source as unborn Lord liberates mortals from delusion and sin.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine as unborn and beginningless, and be freed from delusion.

lessons.text: Share what moves you; it wakes others up.
lessons.rationale: The wise, rooted in the source of all, worship with delight and enlighten one another — the devotional response to divine origin.
lessons.old_text: Absorb your mind in the divine and awaken others by sharing it.

lessons.text: Show up with love, and clarity follows.
lessons.rationale: Loving, steadfast devotion earns the yoga of discrimination that leads to the divine.
lessons.old_text: Worship steadfastly with love, and clear discernment will be given.

lessons.text: Let understanding burn away your confusion.
lessons.rationale: The divine dwelling within destroys ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Let the lamp of knowledge burn away the darkness of ignorance.

lessons.text: The best of anything is a glimpse of something larger.
lessons.rationale: Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or powerful is a fragment of divine splendor.
lessons.old_text: See every glory and power as a spark of the divine splendor.

lessons.text: Meet what overwhelms you with humility, not fear.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna, awestruck and trembling, joins his palms and bows — the human response of humility before the vast.
lessons.old_text: Bow your head before what dwarfs you.

lessons.text: Do your part; the outcome isn't yours to control.
lessons.rationale: 'I am Time, the destroyer; they are already slain — be a mere instrument.' Act your role; the result is not yours to author.
lessons.old_text: Do your part; the outcome is already set.

lessons.text: Face your fight with courage, not dread.
lessons.rationale: Told the outcome is decided, Arjuna is urged to act with courage, not distress: fight and you shall prevail.
lessons.old_text: Fight your battle without fear of losing.

lessons.text: Honor what you've been taking for granted.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna repents for his casual, familiar treatment of Krishna at play and meals, and humbly begs forgiveness.
lessons.old_text: Apologize for the times you took greatness lightly.

lessons.text: Wholehearted attention reveals what routine can't.
lessons.rationale: Not by Vedas, austerity, or sacrifice — but by single-minded devotion alone can the divine be truly known and seen.
lessons.old_text: Only devotion, not ritual, reveals the deepest truth.

lessons.text: Work without clinging, and hold no one as enemy.
lessons.rationale: The one who acts for the Supreme, free from attachment and bearing no hatred toward any being, reaches the divine.
lessons.old_text: Work for the sacred, unattached and free of enmity.

lessons.text: The concrete is easier to hold than the abstract.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna's question and Krishna's verdict: those who fix their minds on a personal, knowable divine with faith are the best in yoga.
lessons.old_text: Loving the personal divine beats worshipping the abstract.

lessons.text: The abstract road is valid, just steeper to walk.
lessons.rationale: Worshippers of the unmanifest also reach the goal by restraining senses and serving all, but their road is steeper for embodied beings.
lessons.old_text: The formless path is valid but harder to walk.

lessons.text: Give yourself fully, and you'll be carried through.
lessons.rationale: Those who offer all actions and rest their minds on the divine are swiftly rescued from the ocean of samsara.
lessons.old_text: Surrender your whole self and be carried across.

lessons.text: Start by giving it your full attention.
lessons.rationale: Top rung of the graded ladder: absorb mind and intellect fully in the divine and dwell there.
lessons.old_text: First, simply fix your mind on the divine.

lessons.text: If focus fails, keep showing up.
lessons.rationale: Second rung: when steady focus fails, use repeated disciplined practice (abhyasa yoga) to draw near.
lessons.old_text: If you cannot hold focus, practice reaching for it.

lessons.text: Can't stay steady? Act on it anyway.
lessons.rationale: Third rung: even without practice, dedicate your actions to the divine and you still attain perfection.
lessons.old_text: If practice is too hard, just work for the divine.

lessons.text: At minimum, let go of needing a reward.
lessons.rationale: Lowest rung of the fallback ladder: renounce the fruits of every action with self-control.
lessons.old_text: If all else fails, at least surrender the results.

lessons.text: Release the outcome and peace arrives at once.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing the fruits of action is ranked above knowledge and meditation because peace immediately follows it.
lessons.old_text: Let go of outcomes and peace arrives at once.

lessons.text: Wish no one harm; be a friend to all.
lessons.rationale: Opens the devotee-qualities list: freedom from hatred, friendliness, and compassion toward every being.
lessons.old_text: Hate no creature; be a friend to all.

lessons.text: Stay steady through highs and lows; forgive quickly.
lessons.rationale: Same verse names a distinct virtue: evenness across pleasure and pain, free of ego, and forgiving.
lessons.old_text: Stay balanced in pleasure and pain, quick to forgive.

lessons.text: Stay content and steady, your resolve firm.
lessons.rationale: The devotee dear to the divine is ever content, steady in meditation, self-controlled, and firm in conviction.
lessons.old_text: Be content and steady, your resolve unshaken.

lessons.text: Trouble no one, and let nothing trouble you.
lessons.rationale: Distinct quality: causing no agitation to others and remaining free from joy, anger, fear, and anxiety.
lessons.old_text: Neither disturb the world nor let it disturb you.

lessons.text: Want less, stay clear, drop the anxious scheming.
lessons.rationale: The devotee is free from wants, pure, capable, unconcerned, and has renounced all anxious undertakings.
lessons.old_text: Want nothing, stay pure, and drop every anxious scheme.

lessons.text: Stop clinging and recoiling; let things be.
lessons.rationale: Neither rejoicing nor hating, grieving nor craving, the devotee renounces both good and evil in devotion.
lessons.old_text: Stop chasing and recoiling; rise above good and bad.

lessons.text: Treat friend and foe, honor and insult, alike.
lessons.rationale: Equanimity toward friend and enemy, honor and dishonor, heat and cold, pleasure and pain, free of attachment.
lessons.old_text: Treat friend and foe, honor and insult, the same.

lessons.text: Let praise and blame pass through you.
lessons.rationale: Closing devotee virtue: equal to censure and praise, silent, satisfied with whatever comes, steady-minded.
lessons.old_text: Take praise and blame alike; be content with anything.

lessons.text: Follow through with faith, all in.
lessons.rationale: Summary verse: those who follow this immortal dharma with faith, making the divine their goal, are exceedingly dear.
lessons.old_text: Walk this path with faith and become truly beloved.

lessons.text: Let desire and pain pass; they aren't you.
lessons.rationale: Lists the field's constituents and modifications, distinct from the knower.
lessons.old_text: Count desire, hatred, and pain as passing field, not self.

lessons.text: Stay humble, honest, and free of pretense.
lessons.rationale: Mines humility, unpretentiousness, and uprightness from the knowledge list.
lessons.old_text: Stay humble, upright, and free of pretense.

lessons.text: Harm no one; forgive; keep yourself in check.
lessons.rationale: Mines non-injury, forgiveness, and self-control as a distinct virtue-cluster.
lessons.old_text: Injure no one; forgive, and hold yourself in check.

lessons.text: Stay humble enough to keep learning.
lessons.rationale: Mines service to the teacher, purity, and steadfastness.
lessons.old_text: Serve your teacher; stay pure and steadfast.
lessons.revoice_note: Reframed 'serve your teacher' as lifelong learning.

lessons.text: Loosen your grip on pleasure and ego.
lessons.rationale: Indifference to sense objects and absence of egoism.
lessons.old_text: Loosen the ego's grip on sense pleasures.

lessons.text: Face aging, sickness, and death clearly.
lessons.rationale: Perceiving the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness, and pain.
lessons.old_text: Face clearly the pain in birth, sickness, and death.

lessons.text: Don't tie your identity to family or home.
lessons.rationale: Non-identification of the Self with son, wife, home, and the rest.
lessons.old_text: Don't bind your identity to family or home.

lessons.text: Stay even whether outcomes please or disappoint.
lessons.rationale: Constant even-mindedness toward the desirable and undesirable.
lessons.old_text: Stay even whether outcomes please or disappoint.

lessons.text: Make room for solitude to hear yourself.
lessons.rationale: Unswerving devotion, resorting to solitude, distaste for crowds.
lessons.old_text: Withdraw into solitude to deepen your devotion.
lessons.revoice_note: Dropped 'devotion'; kept the value of solitude.

lessons.text: Keep coming back to knowing yourself.
lessons.rationale: Constancy in Self-knowledge is declared to be true knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Keep returning your attention to the Self.

lessons.text: Support everything; cling to nothing.
lessons.rationale: Unattached yet supporting all; the experiencer without attachment.
lessons.old_text: Support everything while clinging to nothing.

lessons.text: What you cling to shapes who you become.
lessons.rationale: Attachment to nature's qualities causes birth in good and evil wombs.
lessons.old_text: Notice how clinging to moods shapes your rebirth.

lessons.text: Watch your reactions without meddling in them.
lessons.rationale: The Supreme Soul as observer, permitter, and sustainer within the body.
lessons.old_text: Let the inner witness watch and permit, not meddle.

lessons.text: Grow through reflection, learning, or action.
lessons.rationale: The three paths by which people behold the Self.
lessons.old_text: Reach the Self by meditation, knowledge, or action.
lessons.revoice_note: Reframed the three paths as ways to grow.

lessons.text: Harm others and you harm yourself.
lessons.rationale: Seeing the same Lord everywhere, one does not destroy the Self by the self.
lessons.old_text: Seeing one Self everywhere, you stop harming yourself.

lessons.text: Touch everything; let nothing stain you.
lessons.rationale: Like all-pervading ether, the Self seated in the body is not tainted.
lessons.old_text: Stay unstained like space that touches everything.

lessons.text: Clear understanding sets you free.
lessons.rationale: Opening promise: those who take refuge in this supreme knowledge attain unity and are undisturbed by creation or dissolution.
lessons.old_text: Take refuge in the highest knowledge for freedom.

lessons.text: Three forces drive you: clarity, craving, inertia.
lessons.rationale: Introduces sattva, rajas, tamas as qualities born of nature that bind the embodied self; the chapter's thesis.
lessons.old_text: Recognize the three inborn forces that bind you.

lessons.text: Even clarity and joy can quietly trap you.
lessons.rationale: Sattva-specific: luminous and pure, yet it binds through attachment to happiness and knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Watch how even clarity and joy quietly bind.

lessons.text: Craving chains you to restless action.
lessons.rationale: Rajas-specific: passion, thirst, and attachment that bind through ceaseless action.
lessons.old_text: See how craving chains you to restless action.

lessons.text: Catch the dullness pulling you toward neglect.
lessons.rationale: Tamas-specific: born of ignorance, it binds through heedlessness, indolence, and sleep.
lessons.old_text: Catch the dullness luring you toward sleep and neglect.

lessons.text: Notice which force is driving you right now.
lessons.rationale: Each guna attaches differently and takes turns dominating; the forces are always in flux within you.
lessons.old_text: Notice one force overpower the others, then shift.

lessons.text: When insight sharpens, clarity is leading you.
lessons.rationale: Diagnostic sign of sattva predominant: wisdom-light shining through every gate of the body.
lessons.old_text: When insight lights every sense, harmony is guiding you.

lessons.text: When greed and restlessness surge, craving is driving you.
lessons.rationale: Diagnostic sign of rajas predominant: greed, activity, restlessness, and longing.
lessons.old_text: When greed and restlessness surge, passion is driving you.

lessons.text: When fog and laziness settle, inertia has taken hold.
lessons.rationale: Diagnostic sign of tamas predominant: darkness, inertia, carelessness, and delusion.
lessons.old_text: When fog and laziness settle, inertia has taken hold.

lessons.text: The mood that rules you shapes where you end up.
lessons.rationale: Each guna dominant at death leads to a different destiny: pure worlds, rebirth among the active, or the womb of the deluded.
lessons.old_text: Know the force ruling at death shapes your rebirth.

lessons.text: Every act bears fruit: clarity, pain, or dullness.
lessons.rationale: The fruits of the gunas differ: sattva yields knowledge, rajas yields pain and greed, tamas yields ignorance.
lessons.old_text: Weigh each act's fruit: clarity, pain, or dullness.

lessons.text: Your ruling mood lifts, holds, or sinks you.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic natures ascend, rajasic dwell in the middle, tamasic descend.
lessons.old_text: Your ruling force lifts, holds, or sinks you.

lessons.text: See the forces at work, then rise above them.
lessons.rationale: The seer who beholds no agent but the gunas, and knows what is higher, transcends them to immortality.
lessons.old_text: See only the gunas acting, then rise beyond them.

lessons.text: Stay steady whether moods rise or fade.
lessons.rationale: The marks of one who has transcended the gunas: neither hating nor longing, equal in pleasure and pain, praise and blame, friend and foe.
lessons.old_text: Stay unmoved whether the forces arise or fade.

lessons.text: Steady commitment carries you beyond your moods.
lessons.rationale: Unwavering devotion is the means to cross beyond the gunas and become fit for Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Serve with steady devotion to cross beyond them.

lessons.text: Cut an unhealthy attachment at its root.
lessons.rationale: The upside-down cosmic tree of worldly existence, felled with the strong axe of non-attachment.
lessons.old_text: Cut the tangled tree of attachment at its root.

lessons.text: Rise above pride, delusion, and the pull of opposites.
lessons.rationale: The undeluded conquer attachment and the pairs of pleasure and pain to reach the eternal.
lessons.old_text: Rise above pride, delusion, and the pull of opposites.

lessons.text: Understand what matters and right action follows.
lessons.rationale: Knowing the highest Purusha makes one wise and accomplishes all duties.
lessons.old_text: Know the supreme Self and your duty fulfills itself.

lessons.text: Face danger without fear, and keep your heart clean.
lessons.rationale: Opens the divine list with fearlessness and purity of heart (16:1).
lessons.old_text: Meet danger fearlessly and keep your heart pure.

lessons.text: Master your impulses; give without holding back.
lessons.rationale: Sense-control and almsgiving named among divine endowments in 16:1.
lessons.old_text: Master your senses and give without holding back.

lessons.text: Tell the truth. Harm nothing.
lessons.rationale: Harmlessness and truth head the second stanza of virtues (16:2).
lessons.old_text: Speak the truth and harm no living thing.

lessons.text: Let anger go. Stay gentle, want less.
lessons.rationale: Absence of anger, non-covetousness, and gentleness from 16:2, distinct from truth/non-violence.
lessons.old_text: Let anger go; stay gentle and free of greed.

lessons.text: Forgive quickly. Carry no grudge, no pride.
lessons.rationale: Forgiveness, absence of hatred and pride mark the divine-born nature (16:3).
lessons.old_text: Forgive readily and carry no hatred or pride.

lessons.text: Good character frees you; cruelty traps you.
lessons.rationale: 16:4-6 contrast the two natures: divine leads to liberation, demonic to bondage; framing verses grouped.
lessons.old_text: Choose the divine path; it frees, the demonic binds.

lessons.text: Know what to do, and what to leave alone.
lessons.rationale: The demonic fail to discern right action and refrain (16:7).
lessons.old_text: Know what to do and what to shun.

lessons.text: Don't treat the world as meaningless.
lessons.rationale: The demonic worldview denies truth, morality, and God, breeding destruction (16:8-9).
lessons.old_text: Don't dismiss the world as meaningless and godless.

lessons.text: Drop the pretense and the endless chasing.
lessons.rationale: Insatiable desire, hypocrisy, and anxious hoarding for lust described across 16:10-12.
lessons.old_text: Drop hypocrisy and the endless chase after craving.

lessons.text: Your possessions aren't proof of your worth.
lessons.rationale: The demonic boast of acquisitions, conquests, and status (16:13-15).
lessons.old_text: Stop counting gains as proof of your greatness.

lessons.text: Don't let fantasy drag you under.
lessons.rationale: Bewildered by fancies and snared by delusion, they fall into hell (16:16).
lessons.old_text: Untangle from fancies before delusion drags you down.

lessons.text: Don't make a show of your good acts.
lessons.rationale: Ostentatious, name-only sacrifices contrary to scripture (16:17).
lessons.old_text: Never worship for show or to be seen.

lessons.text: Arrogance curdles into cruelty. Drop it.
lessons.rationale: Egoism and malice breed hatred and a downward spiral of rebirth (16:18-20).
lessons.old_text: Let go of arrogance that turns you hateful.

lessons.text: Shut the three gates of ruin: lust, anger, greed.
lessons.rationale: The signature teaching of the chapter: three gates to hell (16:21).
lessons.old_text: Shut the three gates of ruin: lust, anger, greed.

lessons.text: Free of those, do what truly serves you.
lessons.rationale: Liberation from the three gates lets you pursue the beneficial and reach the goal (16:22).
lessons.old_text: Freed from those gates, do what truly benefits you.

lessons.text: Don't let impulse override hard-won wisdom.
lessons.rationale: Acting on desire while casting aside scripture yields no perfection (16:23).
lessons.old_text: Don't let raw impulse override tested wisdom.

lessons.text: Let sound principles decide your actions.
lessons.rationale: Make scripture your authority for right and wrong action (16:24).
lessons.old_text: Let sound principles decide what you should do.

lessons.text: You become what you believe.
lessons.rationale: 17:3 states the chapter's thesis directly: a person is made of their faith; as their faith is, so are they.
lessons.old_text: You become the faith you hold.

lessons.text: Don't punish yourself to prove your discipline.
lessons.rationale: 17:5-6 condemn austerities born of hypocrisy and lust that senselessly torment body and spirit.
lessons.old_text: Don't torture yourself with ego-driven, harsh discipline.

lessons.text: Eat what brings you health, strength, and calm.
lessons.rationale: 17:8 describes sattvic food that increases life, purity, health, and joy.
lessons.old_text: Eat wholesome food that brings health, strength, and cheer.

lessons.text: Skip food that overstimulates and leaves you wrecked.
lessons.rationale: 17:9 names rajasic foods (bitter, sour, burning) that produce pain, grief, and disease.
lessons.old_text: Avoid harsh, overstimulating food that breeds pain and disease.

lessons.text: Refuse what's stale, spoiled, and lifeless.
lessons.rationale: 17:10 describes tamasic food that is stale, putrid, and impure.
lessons.old_text: Refuse stale, spoiled, and lifeless food.

lessons.text: Do good because it's right, not for reward.
lessons.rationale: 17:11 defines sattvic sacrifice: offered as duty, without desire for reward.
lessons.old_text: Do your work as sacred duty, not for reward.

lessons.text: Good done for reward and display is hollow.
lessons.rationale: 17:12 marks sacrifice done for reward and display as rajasic.
lessons.old_text: Never perform good deeds merely for show.

lessons.text: Respect what's wise; keep yourself clean and gentle.
lessons.rationale: 17:14 lists austerity of the body: reverence for the wise, purity, straightforwardness, non-injury.
lessons.old_text: Honor your teachers and keep your body pure and gentle.

lessons.text: Speak what's true, kind, and calm.
lessons.rationale: 17:15 defines austerity of speech: truthful, pleasant, beneficial, causing no agitation.
lessons.old_text: Speak words that are true, kind, and calming.

lessons.text: Steady your mind with calm and self-control.
lessons.rationale: 17:16 defines mental austerity: serenity, good-heartedness, self-control, purity of nature.
lessons.old_text: Steady your mind with serenity and self-control.

lessons.text: Discipline done for applause won't last.
lessons.rationale: 17:18 marks austerity done for honor and reception, with hypocrisy, as rajasic and unstable.
lessons.old_text: Don't practice discipline just to win praise.

lessons.text: Never turn discipline into self-harm or cruelty.
lessons.rationale: 17:19 condemns tamasic austerity practiced through self-torture or to destroy another.
lessons.old_text: Never turn discipline into self-torture or harm to others.

lessons.text: Give to those who need it, expecting nothing back.
lessons.rationale: 17:20 defines sattvic giving: to a worthy person, at a fitting place and time, expecting nothing back.
lessons.old_text: Give as a duty to those who truly deserve it.

lessons.text: Give generously, never grudgingly or for payback.
lessons.rationale: 17:21 marks giving that expects a reward or is done reluctantly as rajasic.
lessons.old_text: Give generously, never grudgingly or for return.

lessons.text: Give with respect, at the right time and place.
lessons.rationale: 17:22 condemns gifts given at the wrong time and place, to the unworthy, with insult.
lessons.old_text: Give with respect, at the right place and time.

lessons.text: Without conviction, your effort comes to nothing.
lessons.rationale: 17:28 closes the chapter: whatever is done without faith is 'Asat', of no value here or hereafter.
lessons.old_text: Act with faith, or your effort comes to nothing.

lessons.text: Give up the craving, not the work.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's core distinction: sannyasa renounces desire-driven action, tyaga abandons the fruits.
lessons.old_text: Renounce selfish desire, not the action itself.

lessons.text: Don't abandon the practices that better you.
lessons.rationale: These purifying acts should never be abandoned but continued.
lessons.old_text: Keep performing charity, sacrifice, and austerity.

lessons.text: Even good work, do it without clinging to results.
lessons.rationale: Perform good acts while dropping attachment and desire for results.
lessons.old_text: Do even worthy deeds without craving reward.

lessons.text: Never quit your duty out of confusion.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing obligatory action from confusion is tamasic, not true renunciation.
lessons.old_text: Never abandon your duty out of delusion.

lessons.text: Don't quit hard work just to dodge discomfort.
lessons.rationale: Dropping action from fear of bodily trouble is mere rajasic renunciation, earning no merit.
lessons.old_text: Don't quit hard work to avoid discomfort.

lessons.text: Do what's needed simply because it's right.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic renunciation: act because it ought to be done, without attachment or reward.
lessons.old_text: Do required work simply because it's right.

lessons.text: Don't dread hard tasks or crave easy ones.
lessons.rationale: The true renouncer hates no unpleasant task and clings to no pleasant one.
lessons.old_text: Neither dread hard tasks nor crave easy ones.

lessons.text: You can't stop acting, so release the results.
lessons.rationale: No embodied being can abandon all action; relinquishing rewards is real renunciation.
lessons.old_text: You cannot stop acting; release the fruits.

lessons.text: Cling to outcomes and they own you.
lessons.rationale: The threefold fruit accrues after death to those who cling, never to renouncers.
lessons.old_text: Only the unattached escape action's binding fruits.

lessons.text: Many forces shape any outcome, not just you.
lessons.rationale: Body, doer, senses, functions, and the presiding deity are the five causes of every deed.
lessons.old_text: Know that many factors, not you alone, drive action.

lessons.text: Stop taking sole credit for what happens.
lessons.rationale: The untrained mind wrongly views the isolated Self as the agent.
lessons.old_text: Stop seeing yourself as the sole doer.

lessons.text: Act without ego, and nothing binds you.
lessons.rationale: Free of the egoistic notion, one is not bound even by great action.
lessons.old_text: Act free of ego and remain unbound.

lessons.text: See the same thread running through everyone.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic knowledge perceives the undivided Reality present in every being.
lessons.old_text: See one indestructible reality within all beings.

lessons.text: Don't mistake one piece for the whole picture.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic knowledge clings to a single trivial effect as if it were everything.
lessons.old_text: Don't mistake one fragment for the whole.

lessons.text: Do your duty without craving, favoritism, or spite.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic action is ordained, unattached, and free of desire for reward.
lessons.old_text: Do prescribed work without love, hate, or gain.

lessons.text: Weigh the cost and your limits before acting.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic action ignores loss, injury, and one's own capacity.
lessons.old_text: Weigh consequences and your ability before acting.

lessons.text: Work with resolve, unmoved by win or loss.
lessons.rationale: The sattvic doer is firm and enthusiastic, unaffected by outcome.
lessons.old_text: Work with resolve, unmoved by success or failure.

lessons.text: Root out laziness, deceit, and delay.
lessons.rationale: These mark the tamasic doer; naming them helps you shed them.
lessons.old_text: Root out laziness, deceit, and procrastination.

lessons.text: Know clearly what to pursue and what to drop.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic intellect knows action from inaction, bondage from liberation.
lessons.old_text: Discern clearly what to do and avoid.

lessons.text: Don't let confusion make wrong look right.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic intellect, wrapped in darkness, mistakes adharma for dharma.
lessons.old_text: Never let darkness call wrong things right.

lessons.text: Steady your mind, your breath, your senses.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic firmness restrains mind, life-force, and senses by unwavering yoga.
lessons.old_text: Steady your mind, breath, and senses through discipline.

lessons.text: Stop clinging to fear, grief, and laziness.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic firmness refuses to let go of these; releasing them frees you.
lessons.old_text: Stop clinging to sleep, fear, grief, and despair.

lessons.text: Choose the joy that's hard first, sweet later.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic happiness is poison at first, nectar in the end, born of self-realization.
lessons.old_text: Choose joy that starts bitter but ends sweet.

lessons.text: Beware pleasures sweet now, bitter later.
lessons.rationale: Rajasic happiness from sense contact begins as nectar and ends as poison.
lessons.old_text: Beware pleasures sweet now but poisonous later.

lessons.text: Let your natural strengths shape your work.
lessons.rationale: Duties are apportioned according to the qualities born of one's own nature.
lessons.old_text: Let your natural qualities shape your work.

lessons.text: You grow most by mastering your own work.
lessons.rationale: Each person attains perfection by being devoted to their own duty.
lessons.old_text: Reach perfection through devotion to your own work.

lessons.text: Pour your whole heart into ordinary work.
lessons.rationale: Worshipping the source of all through one's own duty leads to perfection.
lessons.old_text: Turn your everyday work into worship.

lessons.text: Better your own path done badly than another's done well.
lessons.rationale: One's own dharma, though flawed, is better than another's performed well.
lessons.old_text: Do your own duty poorly over another's well.

lessons.text: Every job has flaws; don't quit yours over them.
lessons.rationale: Every undertaking has some fault, as fire has smoke; keep to your inborn duty.
lessons.old_text: Don't abandon your calling for its flaws.

lessons.text: Master your wants, and action stops weighing on you.
lessons.rationale: Unattached intellect and self-mastery yield the supreme state of actionlessness.
lessons.old_text: Subdue desire to find freedom in action.

lessons.text: Clear your head; let go of craving and hate.
lessons.rationale: A pure intellect controls the self and relinquishes attraction and aversion.
lessons.old_text: Purify your intellect and drop attraction and hatred.

lessons.text: Live simply, speak less, sit with yourself.
lessons.rationale: Solitude, sparing food, subdued speech, and constant meditation prepare one for Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Live simply, speak little, and meditate steadily.

lessons.text: Let go of ego, arrogance, and 'mine.'
lessons.rationale: Abandoning egoism, arrogance, and possessiveness makes one fit for Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Release ego, arrogance, and the sense of 'mine'.

lessons.text: Grow calm, and treat everyone as equal.
lessons.rationale: Serene in the Self, one neither grieves nor desires and treats all equally.
lessons.old_text: Grow serene and regard all beings alike.

lessons.text: Devote yourself fully, and real understanding follows.
lessons.rationale: By devotion one truly knows the Lord and enters the Supreme.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine through wholehearted devotion.

lessons.text: Do everything, but stay anchored to something steady.
lessons.rationale: Performing all actions while sheltered in the divine grants the eternal state.
lessons.old_text: Take refuge while still doing all your work.

lessons.text: Act fully, then let go of what you did.
lessons.rationale: Mentally renounce all deeds in the Lord and fix the mind on Him.
lessons.old_text: Offer every action mentally to the divine.

lessons.text: Drop the ego, and the obstacles shrink.
lessons.rationale: Fixing the mind on the divine overcomes obstacles; egoism leads to ruin.
lessons.old_text: Surrender your ego to overcome every obstacle.

lessons.text: Your nature will act, whether your ego likes it or not.
lessons.rationale: Refusing to act from egoism is vain; one's own nature will drive the deed anyway.
lessons.old_text: Your nature compels what your ego refuses.

lessons.text: Give yourself fully, and peace follows.
lessons.rationale: Taking full refuge in the Lord yields supreme peace and the eternal abode.
lessons.old_text: Surrender with your whole being to find peace.

lessons.text: Think it through, then decide for yourself.
lessons.rationale: Krishna gives the secret wisdom, then leaves Arjuna free to reflect and act.
lessons.old_text: Reflect deeply, then choose your own path.

lessons.text: Fix your focus, and you'll get there.
lessons.rationale: Devote your mind, worship, and bow, and you will reach the Lord.
lessons.old_text: Fix your mind on the divine and arrive.

lessons.text: Do your best, then stop clinging to the rest.
lessons.rationale: The surrender climax: abandon all duties, take sole refuge, and be freed from sin.
lessons.old_text: Let go of everything, take refuge, stop grieving.

lessons.text: Don't force advice on those who won't hear it.
lessons.rationale: Never teach this to the unwilling; teaching devotees with love is dearest of all.
lessons.old_text: Share sacred wisdom only with the receptive.

lessons.text: Listen openly, without a hostile heart.
lessons.rationale: Hearing it faithfully and without malice leads to liberation and happy worlds.
lessons.old_text: Listen with faith and free from malice.

lessons.text: Listen with full attention, and confusion clears.
lessons.rationale: Krishna asks whether one-pointed attention has destroyed delusion.
lessons.old_text: Listen with full focus to dispel ignorance.

lessons.text: Let clarity return, then act without hesitation.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna's delusion is gone, his doubts dissolved, and he resolves to act.
lessons.old_text: Let clarity return and act without doubt.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: The unreal never is; the real never ceases to be.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Pure metaphysics (transient vs. permanent) with no actionable kernel.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: Bodies perish, but what pervades them cannot be destroyed.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Metaphysics of the indestructible self; not universal daily wisdom.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: The self is never the slayer nor the slain.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Metaphysics of the unkillable self; abstract, not actionable.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: Death exchanges bodies like worn-out clothes for new.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Reincarnation imagery; scriptural cosmology, not modern wisdom.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: No weapon, fire, water, or wind can harm the soul.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Invulnerability-of-soul metaphysics; no human kernel.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: The self is unmanifest and beyond the mind's reach.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Self as unmanifest/unthinkable; pure metaphysics.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: The self is a marvel that few ever truly grasp.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Wonder-of-the-self verse; mystical, not actionable.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: The same deathless self dwells within every being.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Deathless indweller in all beings; metaphysical, not daily wisdom.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: Rest your mind in the flawless real and transcend rebirth.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Only content is resting the mind in Brahman to transcend rebirth—pure metaphysics, and overlaps modernizable mind-rest lessons (132, 134).

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: Worship the one presence dwelling in all beings.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Reduces to worshipping the one presence in all beings—religious framing that adds nothing beyond kept connection lessons (87, 107, 142, 144).

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: Recognize all states of being flow from one.
dropped_in_revoice.reason: Pure guna metaphysics; kernel overlaps 154/159 (moods aren't you). Dropped to avoid redundancy.

dropped_in_revoice.old_text: Seek refuge to understand self, spirit, and action.
